subject: Project Management
title: Toyota vs Ford
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Toyota vs Ford.

hook.text: Ford pushed products through the line as fast as possible. Toyota pulled them through on demand.

buildup.text: Ford's push system built inventory and hoped it would sell. Toyota built only what was ordered.

discovery.text: Push systems create waste: unsold stock, storage costs, and obsolete products.

twist.text: By the 1980s, Toyota's quality and efficiency outpaced all American automakers.

climax.text: The pull vs push debate defined modern manufacturing — and Toyota's pull model won.

punchline.text: Build what's wanted, not what's possible.

quiz.question: What is the key difference between Toyota's and Ford's production models?
options: Toyota used pull (on-demand); Ford used push (build and hope)
Ford was more efficient than Toyota
Toyota copied Ford's assembly line exactly
